title: claim-next skips cells whose files are held by another checkout
action: Extend claimNextCell's hold-free predicate (templates/lib/cells.mjs:1950-1953): after the existing findSessionConflicts check, also consult findForeignHolds(mainRoot, holder, cell.files). Topology inside cells.mjs via resolveRoots (state.mjs already imported by cells.mjs orbit; ordinary checkout => holder 'main', mainRoot = root; linked-valid granted => holder id, mainRoot from resolveRoots; on resolveRoots throw, treat as no foreign holds — claim-next must never crash on topology). A skipped-for-foreign-hold cell is skipped exactly like a local hold today (silent skip, next candidate). Tests: skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_cells.mjs (post cs-2b split of the old test_lib.mjs monolith, this is where claimNextCell's selection-logic tests now live) gets a fabricated ledger entry proving a cell whose files overlap a foreign hold is skipped while a clean cell claims, and that holder-own entries do not skip. Self-onboard mirrors + plugin trees; full verify green.

truths: claimNextCell skips any candidate whose files overlap an unexpired ledger entry held by a different checkout, and still claims candidates overlapping only the acting checkout's own entries.
Topology failure (resolveRoots throw) degrades to today's behavior (no foreign-hold consultation), never a crash.
Repos with no ledger behave byte-identically to today (missing = empty).
Mirrors byte-identical; full run_verify.mjs green including claim race suite.
prohibitions: Do NOT change claim ordering, lane selection, budget checks, or sweepExpiredClaims behavior.
Do NOT make claim-next write to the ledger — read-only consultation.

trace.outcome: claim-next now consults the shared cross-worktree holds ledger read-only via a cells.mjs-local resolveHoldTopology+findForeignHolds; a cell whose files are held by a different checkout is silently skipped, own-checkout entries and missing ledgers behave byte-identically to today
